element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental mechanic for defeating enemies throughout game
Cross large gaps, knock back enemies, retrieve distant items
Setup puzzles and enemy ambushes through coordination
Reveal hidden paths and items in dark sections
Uncover items from soft dirt terrain
Cover gaps to create traversable paths
Unlock doors and create paths through spatial manipulation
Alternative to throwing: environmental hazard exploitation
Passive protection; extra lives with perfect collection streaks

typical_tension_triggers: Screen-clearing enemy requirements before doors open
Limited item inventory (one per player, two in single-player)
Simultaneous two-player death triggers game over
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Puzzle-exploration driven with combat interludes; boss battles punctuate level climax. Tension builds gradually through environmental complexity and enemy placement rather than constant action. Multiplayer coordination requirement creates tension management.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Soft dirt patches (digging for items); dark areas requiring candles; branching paths with optional puzzles
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Collectible fruit scattered throughout levels; reward areas for completing objectives without getting hit

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty; introduces core mechanics (throwing, basic navigation). Beach and village areas establish foundational puzzle and combat patterns.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty increase; haunted castle and cavern introduce environmental hazards, darker navigation, and complex block-sliding puzzles. Multiplayer coordination becomes essential.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained moderate challenge; pirate ship features advanced puzzle combinations and final boss encounter with dynamic throwable objects. Difficulty remains 'fairly low' per Nintendo Power review.
notable_spikes: Each level's boss fight (uses objects in room for variable damage patterns)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level guides toward boss through main path, with optional puzzle branches for item collection. Encourages replaying for optimal fruit accumulation.
Doors remain locked until all enemies on screen are defeated, forcing combat before progression. Creates tension punctuation points.
Puzzles require simultaneous or sequenced actions by both players—one uses bell to lure enemy, other positions throwable. Single-player mode applies AI control to compensate.
Single-item holding (two in single-player) forces tactical choices: carry key, grappling hook, or candle? Creates navigation puzzle layer separate from combat.
Boss encounters use variable throwable objects (barrels, bombs) available in arena. Player must adapt to available resources rather than executing pre-learned pattern.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All boss encounters, culminating in Keelhaul Pete fight (Level 5)
